name: Móra d'Ebre - Flix Meander Loop
name_original: Móra d'Ebre - Ascó - Flix – circular por el meandro de Flix
description: Cycle 35.3 miles (56.7 km) on this moderate road route through Reserva Natural de Sebes i Meandre de Flix, featuring the stunning Flix Meand
region_intro: Road cycling around Móra D'Ebre offers diverse landscapes within the Terres de l'Ebre region, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The terrain features mostly paved surfaces, winding through areas characterized by the Ebro River, natural parks, and varied elevation changes. Cyclists encounter a mix of gentle river-side paths and more challenging routes through hills and historical areas. The region's natural features provide a scenic backdrop for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Móra D'Ebre The most popular road cycling route is Móra d'Ebre - Priorat Hills Loop, a 35.8 miles (57.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain through the scenic Priorat Hills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Miravet – circular from Mora d'Ebre via Miravet and Ginestar, a moderate 32.5 miles (52.4 km) path. This route passes by the historic Miravet Castle, offering views of the Ebro River. Local road cyclists also love the Móra d'Ebre - Flix Meander Loop, a 35.2 miles (56.7 km) trail leading through the picturesque Flix Meander, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Móra D'Ebre is defined by routes along the Ebro River, through natural parks, and over rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Móra D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 1800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Móra D'Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Móra D'Ebre?
A: There are nearly 90 road cycling routes around Móra D'Ebre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from the Ebro River banks to rolling hills and historical areas.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Yes, Móra D'Ebre offers several routes suitable for beginners and families. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are 5 easy routes available. The region also features 'slow cycling' options like the Via Verda, which follows old railway tracks, and the tranquil Camí de Sirga (GR-99 Route) along the Ebro River, perfect for a relaxed pace.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The terrain around Móra D'Ebre is diverse, featuring mostly paved surfaces. You'll find routes winding through areas characterized by the Ebro River, natural parks, and varied elevation changes. Cyclists can encounter a mix of gentle river-side paths and more challenging routes through hills and historical areas, including the scenic Priorat Hills.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miravet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which dominates the Ebro River, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5134983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Móra d’Ebre&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966585832/castillo-de-miravet-circular-from-mora-d-ebre-via-miravet-and-ginestar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miravet – circular from Mora d'Ebre via Miravet and Ginestar&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option to explore the Miravet area.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Móra D'Ebre is home to 42 difficult road cycling routes, providing ample challenge for experienced riders. The region's varied elevation changes and hilly terrain, such as the Priorat Hills, offer demanding climbs and rewarding descents. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40368936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great view of the Ebro River – Som Pass loop from Móra la Nova&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The road cycling routes in Móra D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paved surfaces, and the blend of challenging climbs with scenic river-side paths.

Q: Are there loop routes available for road cycling in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Móra D'Ebre are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962762592/mora-d-ebre-priorat-hills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra d'Ebre - Priorat Hills Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970886808/mora-d-ebre-flix-meander-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra d'Ebre - Flix Meander Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied distances and elevations.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Móra D'Ebre?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather. The Ebro Delta Natural Park is particularly beautiful during bird migration seasons.

Q: Are there routes that offer great viewpoints or scenic overlooks?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. Routes often follow the Ebro River, providing picturesque views, such as those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970886808/mora-d-ebre-flix-meander-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra d'Ebre - Flix Meander Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through the scenic Flix Meander. The castles of Móra d'Ebre and Miravet also offer panoramic views from their elevated positions.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve status of the region?
A: Yes, the Terres de l'Ebre region, including Móra D'Ebre, is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Many cycling routes traverse this area, particularly those around the Ebro Delta Natural Park, allowing you to experience its diverse ecosystems and abundant birdlife. A 110-kilometer tour around the Ebro Delta is specifically designed to showcase this unique natural environment.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historical sites related to the Battle of the Ebro?
A: The region is rich in history, including sites related to the Battle of the Ebro. While specific routes are not detailed in the guide, the 'Battle of the Ebro Landscape' route, spanning 98 kilometers, is known to pass through relevant historical landscapes, allowing cyclists to combine their ride with a historical exploration.

Q: What are the options for parking when road cycling in Móra D'Ebre?
A: Móra D'Ebre, as a central town in the region, typically offers various parking options, including public car parks, from which you can easily access the start points of many routes. For specific route starting points, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot for recommended parking locations.
tips:
Nice views of the meander with the castle of Flix in the background.
The meander extends for almost 5 kilometres, from below the Ribarroja reservoir. The surroundings of the Ebro river can be seen in a very good state of conservation, the vegetation and the great diversity of fauna.
Bridge formed by five arches of different dimensions supported by large pillars that joins the towns of Móra d'Ebre and Móra la Nova.
Old military building of Islamic origin located within the municipality of Móra d'Ebre, in Ribera d'Ebre. The castle enclosure, which is 54 meters high, was walled to defend against possible attacks.

faqs.question: What level of fitness is required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a moderate road cycling route. It covers approximately 56.7 km with about 641 meters of elevation gain, making it suitable for cyclists with good fitness. While mostly well-paved, some segments might be unpaved and more challenging.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. A major highlight is the View of the Flix Meander, where the Ebro river makes a significant bend, often with the castle of Flix in the background. You'll also pass the historic Móra d'Ebre Bridge and the impressive Castle of Móra d’Ebre.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved and more challenging. The ride consists of approximately 40 km of state roads, 13.9 km of other roads, and about 2.81 km of street sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, a good starting point for this circular route is Mora d'Ebre. You can typically find parking in the town center or near the Ebro river to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ebro region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat. Summer can be very hot, so early morning rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this area?
faqs.answer: This route passes through the Reserva Natural de Sebes i Meandre de Flix. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the natural reserve. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so bringing dogs along is generally not recommended due to traffic and the nature of the activity. If you plan to stop at any natural areas, always keep dogs on a leash and follow local regulations for pets in protected zones.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll encounter sections of the E38, Del Santuari de Pinós als Ports, El Camí IT6TR4 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Paissatges de la Batalla de l'Ebre, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the Ebro river?
faqs.answer: The Ebro river surroundings along this route are noted for being in a very good state of conservation. You can expect diverse vegetation and fauna, particularly around the Flix Meander. Keep an eye out for various bird species and other wildlife typical of riverine environments in Catalonia.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like Mora d'Ebre, Asco, and Flix. These towns offer opportunities to stop for food, drinks, or to refill water bottles at local cafes, restaurants, or shops.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, pack essentials like a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and sun protection. Given the distance, consider bringing enough hydration and nutrition to last the duration of your ride.
